# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.
# Category: path_traversal

import * as fs from 'fs'; // File System Module (Nodejs) for reading/writing the file system. It allows you to interact with your filesystem in node JS environment easily by giving a set of functions such readFile(), writeFile() and more which makes it perfect when working on server-side tasks like serving static files, uploads etc..
import * as path from 'path'; // Path module used for manipulating file paths across operating systems.  It provides methods to combine or separate absolute/relative file paths in a portable way with Node's original crypto API (which helps build an application that can create unique names and manage files). So, you will use it as path joining is done here
import express from 'express'; // ExpressJS framework for node.js  used to develop web applications by providing functionalities of routing & middleware on top using its own features/methods like url parsing etc.. It makes the server side functionality easier and faster because all functions have been bundled together in one module which means less overhead
import multer from 'multer'; // MiddleWare for handling multipart forms (files) - a library that helps to handle `multipart/form-data`, you will use it as we are using the middleware here. It handles uploading files during express requests and has features like limiting file size etc..
const app = express();  // Creates an Express application instance with no configuration functions or request handlers (middlewares) yet defined on that particular server /instance/app in our example above which is then customized as per the needs of your API. It helps you to setup and run Nodejs applications by providing functionalities such handling requests & responses, serving static files etc..
